description: Tiny lacy cotyledons barely visible above the soil.
A low-growing rosette of finely divided, lacy, fern-like green leaves.
A tall, solid green stem covered in fine hairs rises from the lacy rosette of leaves.
The hairy stem is topped with flat umbels of small white flowers, featuring a single dark purple flower in the center.
The flower umbels have folded inward to form a concave 'bird's nest' shape, holding small bristly seeds. The stalk is browning.
A pale whitish taproot, fleshy and smelling strongly of carrot.
A pale whitish taproot, split, hard, and extremely woody.
Finely divided, lacy, fern-like green leaves.
A sturdy, solid green stem covered in fine hairs.
A flat umbel of tiny white flowers, with a single dark purple floret in the exact center.
Small, strongly ribbed seeds covered in tiny bristles, gathered in a dry 'bird's nest' cup.
